title: Aperture Array: Exposure-Block Scheduling
strategies: next-fit online: keep one open exposure block, flush and open a fresh block whenever the next request does not fit; reproduces the evaluator's weak baseline and wastes capacity on fragmentation
first-fit online: for each arriving request scan all open blocks in order and drop it into the first with room, opening a new block only when none fits; reuses earlier gaps without reordering

best-fit-decreasing: sort requests by descending duration, then place each into the block whose remaining room is smallest but still sufficient (tightest fit), leaving big gaps free for later large requests; near-optimal packing well above first-fit
invalid: assign every request to block 0 (or otherwise overflow a block), producing an over-capacity packing that the evaluator rejects to 0
